sessions.intent: Write failing-test contract for the 5 stages of typed-action-returns (Branch A)

before.assumptions: Architect plan v3.3 + stages.md are locked. Stages 0-4 ship Branch A; Branch B is separate. Tests are signatures-only with Assert.Fail / throw 'not implemented'. C# under PLang.Tests/App/TypedReturnsTests/, PLang under Tests/TestModule/TypedReturns/.
before.risk: Build() interface + named-channels are new infra — easy to mis-shape the tests if coder's exact API names differ. Mitigation: test against documented contract surfaces (interface presence, return shape), not internal naming.

actions.context: Stage 0 foundation infra: PlangType removal (6), Build() interface (8), named channels + BuildWarning (9), Data materialization API (7). 30 C# unit signatures total.
Stage 1 tester/File → tester/Test rename. 6 C# units verify type existence, field carryover, and absence of old name everywhere.

Stage 2 mechanical typings — 10 handlers split into two batches. Each handler covered for (a) Run() signature shape, (b) Modules.Describe() catalog return-line, (c) no Data<object> double-wrap. 19 C# units total.

Stage 3 HTTP Response record (6) + runtime Content-Type body dispatch (8) — covers all four fields, the four content-type branches (json/text/binary/missing/text-fallback/csv-materializer), and that http.download is exempt.

Stage 4 per-action Build() impls (11) + (type) hint Compile rule + multi-segment GetByExtension + precedence (8). The precedence file is the trickiest: distinguishing explicit-object from default-object is called out explicitly.

12 PLang .test.goal files — the user-visible end-to-end contract. Each pins a single behaviour (trace-snapshot annotation, build warning surfacing, hint precedence, lazy materialization). Bodies are throw 'not implemented'.
Plan + verdict + summary per character-file workflow.

after.health: PLang.Tests builds clean (0 errors). 88 C# unit signatures + 12 PLang behaviour goals = 100 failing tests staged for coder.
after.notes: User approved all 11 batches in a single pass. No blockers. Recommended next bot: coder, starting at Stage 0.

sessions.intent: Review Stage 0 production code on typed-action-returns

before.assumptions: Stage 0 = 6 coder commits c4404b9c5..3c8285760: IClass interface, Build() pass in validate, named channels w/ noop, slim [PlangType], public Data.As(string), generator SetAction. Coder handoff says 30/30 Stage 0 tests green.
before.risk: Diff is small but architecturally dense — generator emission, attribute redesign, validate hook, materialization API split.

actions.context: Read each file, ran 5 passes. Findings are all LOW severity polish. Real cleanup: AllowMultiple=false vs Registry's multi-attr loop is dead code + stale doc.

after.health: green — no blocker; Stage 0 tests green per handoff; findings are polish/follow-up.
after.notes: 8 findings: 7 LOW + 1 design call (IClass.SetAction default). Most actionable: [PlangType] AllowMultiple=false contradicts Registry comment+loop.
